fallback_from.reason: non-PTY opencode run timed out after one reasonable bounded attempt with only partial edits

summary: OpenCode was invoked with exactly opencode-go/glm-5.1 but timed out after partial provider edits. Hermes completed the bounded backend provider retry task directly: LLM/TTS providers now parse max_retries and retry_backoff_ms options, rebuild POST requests for each retry attempt, retry transient transport/429/5xx failures with bounded exponential backoff, and preserve non-retryable 4xx API error details.

tests.notes: OpenCode used the required model but the foreground non-PTY command timed out after partial edits; Hermes preserved useful edits and completed directly.
Hermes frontend validation passed with no TypeScript output.
Expo web export passed; existing expo-av deprecation warning remains.
The go binary is not installed in this Hermes environment, per prerequisite check.

git.commit_message_suggestion: fix(provider): add bounded retries for OpenAI-compatible calls

blockers: Go tests unavailable because go is not installed.
risks: Go provider tests were added but could not be executed in this environment; review was static plus frontend validation.
follow_up_suggestions: Run the new Go provider tests in CI or a host with Go 1.24.12 installed.
Implement persistent requeue/resume handling for failed segmentation and synthesis segments as a separate backend task.

questions: Does the retry helper preserve request bodies and secret handling across retry attempts?
Are non-retryable API errors still surfaced without repeated calls?
